posts.raw_content:
A Manifesto for the Restoration of Truth

We did not arrive here by accident.

What we face is not a loose collection of slogans or a spontaneous cultural shift. It is the deliberate, multi-generational project of a small group of secular intellectuals who, in the 1920s and 1930s, set out to do what economic Marxism could not: destroy the Christian moral and cultural order of the West from within.

They called it Critical Theory.

They founded the Institute for Social Research in Frankfurt in 1923. When the Nazis rose, they relocated to Columbia University in New York. There, Max Horkheimer, Theodor Adorno, Herbert Marcuse, and their circle rewrote Marxism. They abandoned the factory floor and turned their weapons on the family, the church, objective truth, individual conscience, and the very idea that a man is accountable first to God and then to his own merit.

Antonio Gramsci gave them the strategy: the “long march through the institutions.” Capture the schools, the media, the corporations, the law. Do not seize power by force. Subdue the culture so that the old order collapses under its own weight.

Later operators perfected the delivery system: SEL in classrooms to replace moral formation with emotional compliance; CRT and DEI to replace merit and color-blind justice with group grievance; ESG to replace fiduciary duty with ideological scorekeeping. The United Nations Sustainable Development Goals, the World Economic Forum’s stakeholder capitalism, and the largest foundations and asset managers provided the funding and the global enforcement layer.

The goal was never hidden. Their own writings declare it: Christianity’s pillars — sin and redemption, the nuclear family, transcendent moral law, individual dignity before God — were the enemy. They said so explicitly in Dialectic of Enlightenment, Eros and Civilization, One-Dimensional Man, and the Prison Notebooks.

They nearly succeeded.

But ideas this corrosive always produce their own antidote. The West is waking up. Parents are pulling children from indoctrination. Shareholders are rejecting ESG theater. Voters are rejecting the bureaucratic class that grew rich enforcing it. The emperor has no clothes, and the bill for the last sixty years of engineered decay is coming due.

We do not answer lies with violence. We answer them with truth.

We return to the pillars they sought to destroy:

- The Bible as the final authority on human nature, morality, and justice.
- Merit as the only just measure of a man.
- Free speech as the non-negotiable condition for discovering truth.
- Rule of law that treats every individual as equal before God and the state.
- The creative power of ordered liberty, including cheap and abundant energy, to lift the poor and steward creation.

These are not “right-wing” ideas. They are the proven foundations of human flourishing. Every society that has abandoned them has declined. Every society that has recovered them has risen.

This is not a call to hatred. It is a call to clarity.

Reject the ideology in your home, your school, your workplace, your vote, and your investments. Teach your children the truth. Support institutions that still honor merit and moral order. Speak plainly, without apology.

The ideas were engineered. They can be un-engineered.

The death of these lies is not the end of the West. It is the beginning of its restoration.

Share this. Live it. Build what they tried to tear down.

The pillars still stand. We simply have to return to them.

posts.title: Call to Arms
